text: Commit messages in git should be more helpful: After the task ID, include a colon and the name of the task itself

proposedTasks.title: Include task title in all orchestrator commit messages
proposedTasks.description: Update the orchestrator's git commit queue to include the task/issue title after the task ID in all commit messages, separated by a colon. Currently PRD §5.9 defines patterns like `beads: <summary>` and `merge: opensprint/<task-id> — <task title>`. The merge pattern already includes the title, but other commit types (beads JSONL exports, status transitions) may only reference IDs. Ensure every commit message that references a task ID also includes `: <task title>` immediately after the ID. Acceptance criteria: (1) All orchestrator-generated commits that reference a bead ID include the task title after the ID in the format `<task-id>: <task title>`; (2) Beads export commits summarize which tasks changed by name, not just ID; (3) Existing commit message patterns for PRD updates and worktree merges remain consistent with the new convention; (4) Unit tests verify commit message formatting for each commit type in the queue.

taskTitles: Include task title in all orchestrator commit messages
